title: quercus erythrolepis
intro: Quercus erythrolepis, also known as the Japanese red-barked oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to Japan. The bark of this tree contains tannins, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Quercus erythrolepis has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, lower cholesterol levels, and improve overall cardiovascular health. The bark is typically harvested for herbal preparations, such as tea, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
